author: gbolahan
content:
Hey folks! After trying to contribute some state-diff and backfill PRs to Grandine and Lighthouse, I kept wishing I could do some interesting things to work with ERA/ERA1 archives. Most of the existing support lives inside client codebases or libraries, and I wanted something I could just `cargo install` and use.

So I built a standalone Rust toolkit called `brandon`. It can read, random-access, build, verify, and convert Ethereum ERA archives.

The v0.1 : if you ever need to quickly extract a block, strip receipts, or rebuild a stale index on an archive, it's a one-liner now:

```bash
brandon read mainnet-00000-5ec1ffb8.era1 --slot 42 --format raw -o block.bin
brandon convert strip file.era1 -o slim.era1 --strip-receipts
```

It's client-agnostic, and the library is designed to stay lightweight—the ERA1→ERA converter, for example, lets you bring your own SSZ library instead of forcing a particular consensus implementation.

Install with:

```bash
cargo install brandon
```

Repo: https://github.com/galadd/brandon

If anyone has ERA archives from Erigon, Caplin, or other clients lying around, I'd love to know if Brandon breaks on them or if there are edge cases I've missed.

(Long-term I'm aiming for an `ffmpeg`-style UX where you can do something like `brandon block 12345 --json`, but I'm trying to get the foundation solid first.)
